title: Publisher Confirms, Windows, Failure Ambiguity, and Republish
description: A producer opens a normal channel, calls waitForConfirms after every message, discards sequence identity, and blindly republishes all work after a connection loss even when the broker may already own it. Learners cumulatively produce confirm-window and ambiguous-publication ledger through theory, guided work, debugging, transfer, retrieval, and assessment.
objectives: Use confirm channels and account for callback results, waitForConfirms, nack, return ordering, close, and unresolved publication state.
Bound unconfirmed count, bytes, and age while preserving throughput, memory, fairness, and per-message outcome evidence.
Explain when confirms occur for unroutable messages, persistent classic queues, quorum queues, and multiple routed queues.
